g.data-narration: Welcome! This lecture covers different types of energy essential for understanding physical and computing systems.
g.text: Foundation for CSE: Computer Architecture, Embedded Systems, Power Electronics

g.data-narration: Energy understanding is crucial for CSE engineers to design efficient systems, optimize algorithms, and develop sustainable computing solutions.
Energy exists in multiple forms: kinetic, potential, thermal, chemical, electrical, and nuclear. All can transform from one to another.
Kinetic energy is energy of motion. A moving car, spinning fan, or flowing water all possess kinetic energy proportional to mass and velocity squared.
Rotational kinetic energy applies to spinning objects. A rotating wheel or flywheel stores energy based on its moment of inertia and angular velocity.
g.text: I = moment of inertia, ω = angular velocity
Flywheels, Gyroscopes, Hard disk drives, Electric motors

g.data-narration: Gravitational potential energy depends on height in a gravitational field. A book on a shelf or water in a dam stores this energy due to its position.
Elastic potential energy is stored in stretched or compressed springs. The energy increases with the square of displacement from equilibrium.
Thermal energy is the internal kinetic energy of particles. Temperature measures average kinetic energy, while heat is energy transfer due to temperature difference.
g.text: Heat is energy TRANSFER, thermal energy is

g.data-narration: Chemical energy is stored in atomic bonds. When bonds break or form during reactions, this energy releases as heat, light, or electrical energy.
g.text: Batteries for laptops/phones, Fuel cells, UPS systems
Green energy storage, IoT device power

g.data-narration: Electrical energy powers all modern computing. It flows through circuits and can be calculated using charge and potential difference.
Nuclear energy comes from the immense binding energy in atomic nuclei. Einstein's E=mc² shows tiny mass conversions release enormous energy.
Work is energy transfer through force and displacement. The work-energy theorem states that net work equals change in kinetic energy.
g.text: Work causes change in kinetic energy

g.data-narration: The Law of Conservation of Energy states energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed. This principle governs all physical systems.
Here are all the key formulas we covered. Remember these for solving numerical problems in exams.
Remember: energy cannot be destroyed, only transformed. Watch your sign conventions, use SI units, and understand direction of forces for work.
g.text: Energy cannot be created or destroyed - only transformed
Joule (J) is SI unit - 1 J = 1 kg·m²/s²
